skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide and helper scripts for creating, validating, and packaging AI skills with reusable instructions, references, scripts, and assets.
skill.summary: Create well-structured AI skills with reusable workflows, resources, validation, and packaging guidance.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but the weak-crypto, license URL, and markdown backtick findings are false positives after context review. The skill does include executable helper scripts that create files, chmod a generated script, read SKILL.md, and package directory contents into zip archives. No malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence was found, but publication should note local filesystem and archive-packaging risks.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Filesystem Writes During Skill Initialization
medium_findings.description: The initializer builds a destination from user-provided path and skill name values, then creates directories and files and marks the generated example script executable. This is legitimate skill scaffolding, but it can write outside the intended workspace if callers pass unsafe paths or skill names.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and chmod behavior is direct and confirmed. Risk depends on whether callers provide trusted path and skill name values.

medium_findings.title: Archive Packaging May Include Unintended Local Files
medium_findings.description: The packager recursively includes every file under the selected skill folder in the output zip. This is normal packaging behavior, but users can accidentally include secrets, generated artifacts, or symlinked local files when packaging untrusted folders.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit. The sensitive-file impact depends on the contents of the skill folder being packaged.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches occur in ordinary prose, markdown examples, and validator text rather than cryptographic operations. No hash, cipher, or password-handling implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation and validation strings at the flagged lines, not cryptographic API usage. This strongly supports false-positive classification.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: The static shell-execution findings in SKILL.md are markdown command examples and inline filenames. The Python initializer lines flagged for backticks are inside generated template strings, not runtime command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is static documentation or template text. No subprocess, shell, eval, or os.system call was found in the reviewed scripts.

low_findings.title: Apache License URLs Are Not Runtime Network Activity
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URLs are Apache License reference links. They are license text, not code that performs network requests or transmits data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are inside standard Apache License text. There is no network library usage associated with these lines.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: Review did not find suspicious phrases that claim system authority, request skipped analysis, or instruct the model to ignore existing instructions. The skill content is instructional but does not attempt to override evaluator rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search and manual review found normal workflow guidance. Confidence is high, though no manual review can prove absence of every possible phrasing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Writes From Caller-Provided Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer writes a new skill tree under caller-provided path inputs and creates an executable example script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction and write calls are explicit. The pattern is useful but requires trusted inputs and workspace awareness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Zip Packaging
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager walks the selected skill directory and adds every file it finds to a distributable zip archive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive rglob and zip write behavior is direct. Data exposure risk depends on the packaged folder contents.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent skill packages that are easy for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use. This skill provides a repeatable process, templates, validation checks, and packaging steps for reusable AI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides authors through examples, planning, initialization, editing, packaging, and iteration.
Provides an initializer script that creates a template skill folder with example resources.
Provides a validator that checks basic frontmatter and naming requirements.
Provides a packaging script that validates a skill before creating a zip archive.
Describes how to organize scripts, references, and assets for progressive disclosure.
limitations: Validation is basic and does not prove semantic quality or safety.
Generated templates still require human editing and review.
Packaging can include unintended files if the skill folder is not clean.
The helper scripts assume a local Python environment.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Turn repeated team workflows into a reusable skill with clear instructions and supporting resources.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Use the template and guidance to keep frontmatter, folders, and resource references consistent.

use_cases.title: Package Skills for Sharing
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill and create a distributable archive for review or installation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a skill for a repeated workflow. Ask for the minimum examples needed, then propose the SKILL.md outline and resource folders.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill scaffold for my workflow. Use the initializer pattern, then list which generated files I should keep, edit, or remove.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill for clarity, trigger quality, progressive disclosure, and unnecessary resources. Suggest focused edits before packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Marketplace Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill before release. Check metadata, security-sensitive scripts, resource references, validation output, packaging risk, and user-facing documentation.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for monthly finance reporting.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill plan with trigger description, workflow sections, required reference documents, and scripts to automate repeated report checks.

output_examples.input: Review my draft skill before I package it.
output: A concise review of missing metadata and unclear instructions.
A list of resources to keep, move, or delete.
Packaging cautions for local files and sensitive data.

output_examples.input: Help me turn this process into a reusable AI workflow.
output_examples.output: A structured authoring workflow that starts with examples, identifies reusable assets, drafts instructions, and defines validation steps.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before writing the skill instructions.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move detailed references into separate files.
Inspect the skill folder for secrets or extra files before packaging.
anti_patterns: Do not package generated caches, credentials, or unrelated project files.
Do not rely on vague trigger descriptions that apply to many unrelated tasks.
Do not keep example scripts or assets that the final skill does not use.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skill packages with instructions, metadata, scripts, references, assets, validation, and packaging guidance.

faq.question: Does it write files?
faq.answer: Yes. The initializer creates a local skill folder and example files at the path selected by the caller.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No runtime network access was found. The only URLs reviewed are Apache License reference links.

faq.question: Can it validate a finished skill?
faq.answer: It includes a basic validator for frontmatter, naming format, and description formatting.

faq.question: Can it package a skill for distribution?
faq.answer: Yes. The packaging script validates the skill, then creates a zip archive from the selected skill folder.

faq.question: What should users check before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should remove secrets, unused generated files, local caches, and any files that should not be shared.
